landing.faq.title: 常见问题
landing.faq.q1: AI 回答的准确度如何？
landing.faq.a1: DocTalk 使用最先进的 AI 模型配合检索增强生成技术。每个回答都包含编号引用，链接到确切的原文，您可以一键验证准确性。
landing.faq.a2: DocTalk 支持文本 PDF、扫描文档（通过 OCR）、研究论文、财务报告、合同、手册等。支持最大 50MB、500 页的文件。
landing.faq.a3: 当然。您的文档传输时 TLS 加密，存储时 AES-256 加密，绝不用于 AI 训练，您可以随时删除。我们遵循隐私优先原则。
landing.faq.q4: 可以使用哪些 AI 模型？
landing.faq.a4: DocTalk 提供 3 种性能模式 — 快速、均衡和深度 — 每种模式使用不同的 AI 模型，针对不同需求进行优化。免费用户可使用快速和均衡两种模式。升级至 Plus 可解锁深度分析模式。
landing.faq.a5: 有！免费账户每月包含 500 积分，足够数十个问题使用。无需信用卡即可开始。
landing.faq.a6: 可以。DocTalk 可处理最多 500 页的文档，通过智能分块和语义搜索，确保即使在超长文档中也能给出准确回答。

pricing.headline: 围绕真实文档工作设计的定价，而非模糊的 AI 限制。
pricing.description: DocTalk 专为需要从 PDF、DOCX、幻灯片、电子表格和 URL 中获取准确答案的用户设计。所有方案均包含来源引用和 OCR 支持。更高级别的方案主要扩展使用量限制、解锁深度模型，并为更重度的研究和运营需求添加导出或自定义提示词工作流。

pricing.comparison.title: 方案之间的实际差异
pricing.comparison.description: 所有方案使用相同的核心产品：上传文档、用自然语言提问、通过来源引用验证答案。主要区别在于使用量限制和高级工作流。免费版足以了解 DocTalk 的工作方式。Plus 是活跃每周使用的实用默认选择。Pro 适合经常处理大文件、更多文档和可重复提示词工作流的用户。

about.headline: AI 文档对话，为可验证的答案而设计。
about.description: DocTalk 帮助用户更快地阅读密集文档，同时不牺牲可追溯性。上传 PDF、DOCX、PPTX、XLSX、TXT、Markdown 文件或公开 URL，用自然语言提问，一键跳转到引用的原文段落。
about.optimizeFor.title: 我们优化的方向
about.optimizeFor.item1: 可验证的答案，引用指向原始源文本。
about.optimizeFor.item2: 支持真实文档工作流，不仅仅是 PDF。
about.optimizeFor.item3: 快速上手，包括注册前即可使用的公开演示。
about.optimizeFor.item4: 上传文件和账户数据的隐私优先处理。
about.whoUses.title: 谁在使用 DocTalk
about.whoUses.item1: 审阅论文和技术资料的学生和研究人员。
about.whoUses.item2: 处理长篇、细节密集文档的法律、金融和人力资源团队。
about.whoUses.item3: 需要从合同、演示文稿和报告中快速获取答案的运营者和创业者。
about.whoUses.item4: 任何需要 AI 帮助但不想失去核查来源能力的人。
about.trust.title: 信任、隐私和产品方向
about.trust.paragraph1: DocTalk 基于检索增强生成构建，使答案始终扎根于上传的文档。产品支持引用高亮、多种性能模式，以及跨多种格式的文档工作流。上传的文档在传输和存储时均加密，用户可以从应用中管理或删除数据。
about.trust.paragraph2: 当前公开产品专注于英文 SEO 页面，而应用界面支持 11 种语言。随着产品发展，产品更新、对比和实用指南会在博客上发布。
about.howItWorks.title: 产品工作原理
about.howItWorks.description: 在界面背后，DocTalk 结合了文档解析、分块、检索和语言模型生成，使答案与原始文件保持关联。这种架构很重要，因为产品并非试图替代源文档，而是试图缩短从问题到文档中可验证证据的路径。
about.whatWePublish.title: 我们发布的内容
about.whatWePublish.description: DocTalk 的公开页面专注于实用的产品教育：功能解释、使用场景、软件对比以及关于 AI 文档工作流的博客文章。目标很直接：帮助用户了解产品的优势所在、引用的重要性，以及工具实际适合的工作流。

contact.description: 最快的联系方式是电子邮件。我们使用统一的收件箱处理支持、隐私和产品反馈，确保请求不会在多个渠道中丢失。

useCasesFinance.heroDescription: Upload 10-K filings, earnings reports, and investor presentations. Ask questions in plain language and get AI answers with citations pointing to exact figures and sections.
useCasesFinance.challenge.title: The Financial Analysis Challenge
useCasesFinance.challenge.p1: Financial analysis demands extracting precise data from dense, lengthy documents. A typical 10-K filing from a large public company runs 100 to 300 pages, packed with financial statements, footnotes, risk factor disclosures, management discussion and analysis (MD&A), and legal boilerplate. Quarterly 10-Q filings add another layer of data points every three months. Analysts covering multiple companies may need to review thousands of pages each earnings season.
useCasesFinance.challenge.p2: The critical information is often buried deep. Revenue recognition policy changes might be disclosed in footnote 14 on page 87. A material litigation risk might appear in a single paragraph of the risk factors section. A change in accounting estimates could be mentioned in the MD&A section with an impact quantified only in a supplemental table. Missing these details can mean missing a material investment risk.
useCasesFinance.challenge.p3: Earnings call transcripts present a different challenge. They are conversational in format, often 30 to 50 pages of dialogue between executives and analysts. Finding the specific comment where the CFO discussed margin expectations, or where the CEO addressed a competitive threat, requires reading or searching through the entire transcript.
useCasesFinance.challenge.p4: Time pressure compounds these challenges. During earnings season, analysts need to process new filings within hours, not days. Investment decisions hinge on rapid, accurate extraction of key data points. A tool that accelerates this process while maintaining verifiability directly impacts investment decision quality.
useCasesFinance.howItHelps.title: How DocTalk Helps Financial Analysts
"What was the revenue growth rate year over year?" or "What was the operating margin for Q3?" DocTalk locates the relevant financial data in the document and returns the answer with a citation pointing to the exact table or paragraph.
"How did Q3 revenue compare to Q2?" or "What changed in the debt structure since last year?" Compare figures across sections of the same filing, with citations to both data points so you can verify the comparison.
"What are the main risk factors?" or "Are there any new risk disclosures this quarter?" DocTalk extracts and summarizes risk factor sections, with each risk cited to its location in the filing.
useCasesFinance.features.findDisclosures: "Are there any related-party transactions?" or "What is the revenue recognition policy?" Navigate directly to specific disclosures buried in footnotes and supplementary sections.

useCasesFinance.realWorld.title: Real-World Financial Use Cases
useCasesFinance.realWorld.annualReport.p: An equity analyst covering a technology company uploads the 200-page 10-K filing and asks: "What was the total revenue and year-over-year growth rate?" DocTalk returns the figures with a citation pointing to the consolidated statements of operations on page 54. A follow-up question, "What are the main risk factors related to competition?" surfaces three specific risk factor paragraphs from pages 18 through 22, each with its own citation.

The analyst then asks: "What was the change in deferred revenue from the prior year?" DocTalk locates the balance sheet line item and the related footnote explaining the change. In fifteen minutes of targeted questioning, the analyst has extracted the key data points that would have taken an hour or more of manual reading.
useCasesFinance.realWorld.earningsCall.p: After an earnings call, an analyst uploads the transcript and asks: "What guidance did management provide for next quarter?" DocTalk identifies the guidance comments from the CFO's prepared remarks and the Q&A session, providing citations to the specific dialogue. "What did the CEO say about the competitive landscape?" surfaces the relevant exchange between an analyst and the CEO.

This workflow is especially valuable during earnings season when analysts need to process multiple transcripts in a single day. Instead of reading each 40-page transcript end to end, they can ask the same set of targeted questions across multiple company calls and compare the responses.
useCasesFinance.realWorld.quarterlyCompa: A portfolio manager uploads a company's Q2 and Q3 10-Q filings in separate sessions. For each, they ask the same set of questions: "What was the gross margin?" "What were the operating expenses by category?" "Were there any impairment charges?" The cited answers from each quarter are compiled into a comparison, with every data point traceable to the specific filing and page. This structured approach to quarterly tracking ensures nothing is missed and every figure is verifiable.
useCasesFinance.realWorld.dueDiligence.p: A private equity associate performing due diligence on a potential acquisition target uploads the target's financial statements, shareholder agreements, and key contracts. Targeted questions like "What are the outstanding debt obligations?", "Are there any change-of-control provisions?", and "What is the customer concentration risk?" surface relevant information from across multiple documents. Each finding is citation-backed, making it straightforward to compile into a due diligence report that partners can verify.
useCasesFinance.whyCitations.title: Why Cited Answers Matter for Finance
useCasesFinance.whyCitations.p1: Financial analysis lives and dies by accuracy. A revenue figure off by a few percentage points changes a valuation model. A missed footnote about an accounting policy change can invalidate an entire analysis. In this context, an AI tool that generates answers without verifiable sources is a liability, not an asset.
useCasesFinance.whyCitations.p2post: ensures that every number, every claim, and every summary is traceable to a specific passage in the original document. Click a citation, and the document viewer scrolls to the exact source text and highlights it. You see the original table, the original footnote, the original paragraph. No guessing, no trust required.
useCasesFinance.whyCitations.p3: This verification capability is what distinguishes DocTalk from general-purpose AI chatbots in a financial analysis workflow. The AI accelerates the search process, but the analyst retains full control over verification. Every figure that goes into a model or a memo has a traceable source in the original filing.
useCasesFinance.excel.title: Direct Excel Spreadsheet Support
useCasesFinance.excel.p1: Unlike most AI document tools that only handle PDFs, DocTalk natively supports XLSX file uploads. Upload a financial model, a budget comparison spreadsheet, or a data export directly. The AI processes the tabular data and responds to questions about specific figures, trends, and calculations.
useCasesFinance.excel.p2: Ask "What is the total in column D?" or "Which quarter had the highest revenue?" DocTalk references the specific cells and sheets in its citations. This eliminates the need to convert spreadsheets to PDF or manually describe data to an AI chatbot.
useCasesFinance.excel.p3: For financial professionals who work extensively with Excel, this is a significant workflow improvement. Upload the actual working file, ask questions, and get answers grounded in the real data.

useCasesFinance.faq.q1.answer: Yes. Upload a 10-K filing as a PDF and ask questions like "What was the revenue for fiscal year 2025?" or "What are the main risk factors?" DocTalk extracts the relevant information with numbered citations pointing to the exact section.

useCasesLawyers.heroTitle: AI-Powered Legal Document Analysis with Verifiable Citations
useCasesLawyers.heroDescription: Review contracts, court filings, and regulatory documents faster with AI that cites exact clauses. Every answer links to the source text so you can verify before you rely.
useCasesLawyers.challenge.title: The Legal Document Challenge
useCasesLawyers.challenge.p1: Legal work is, at its core, document work. A single corporate transaction can involve hundreds of contracts, disclosures, and regulatory filings. A litigation matter may require reviewing thousands of pages of court records, depositions, and correspondence. The volume is staggering, and the stakes of missing a critical clause are enormous.
useCasesLawyers.challenge.p2: Due diligence for an acquisition means systematically reviewing corporate documents to identify risks: unusual liability provisions, change-of-control clauses, pending litigation disclosures, intellectual property assignments, and employment agreements with non-compete provisions. Each document must be read carefully because a single overlooked clause can have millions of dollars in consequences.
useCasesLawyers.challenge.p3: Contract review is equally demanding. A 60-page commercial lease contains dozens of interdependent provisions. Termination clauses reference indemnification sections, which in turn reference insurance requirements. Understanding the full picture requires cross-referencing multiple sections, a task that is time-consuming even for experienced attorneys.
useCasesLawyers.challenge.p4: The billable-hour model adds economic pressure. Associates spend significant portions of their time on document review work that, while essential, is repetitive and slow. Any tool that accelerates document review without sacrificing accuracy directly impacts a firm's efficiency and profitability.
useCasesLawyers.howItHelps.title: How DocTalk Helps Legal Professionals

useCasesLawyers.features.filingSummariza: Upload a court filing and ask "Summarize the plaintiff's key arguments" or "What precedents are cited?" Get a structured summary with citations to the exact paragraphs in the filing.

useCasesLawyers.realWorld.title: Real-World Legal Use Cases
useCasesLawyers.realWorld.contractReview: Contract Review: Indemnification, Termination, and Liability

An associate reviewing a 45-page commercial lease uploads the PDF to DocTalk and asks: "Find all indemnification clauses." DocTalk returns three results, each with a numbered citation pointing to the exact section. Citation [1] links to Section 14.2, a broad mutual indemnification provision. Citation [2] links to Section 14.3, a carve-out for environmental liability. Citation [3] links to Exhibit B, an additional indemnification related to pre-existing conditions.

The associate then asks: "What are the termination conditions?" and "Is there a cap on liability?" Each answer references specific clauses with page numbers, allowing the associate to build a clause summary in minutes rather than hours. The citations make it easy to verify the AI's extraction against the actual contract language.
useCasesLawyers.realWorld.dueDiligenceCa: Due Diligence: Analyzing Corporate Document Stacks

During a mid-market acquisition, a legal team needs to review a virtual data room containing corporate bylaws, shareholder agreements, key contracts, and employment agreements. Each document is uploaded individually, and the team runs a consistent set of questions across all of them: "Are there any change-of-control provisions?" "List all non-compete or non-solicitation clauses." "Are there any pending or threatened litigation disclosures?"

DocTalk surfaces relevant provisions from each document with exact citations. The team compiles findings into a due diligence memo, with each finding traceable to a specific document and section. What traditionally takes a team of associates two weeks can be significantly accelerated, with the AI handling initial document screening and the attorneys focusing on legal analysis of the identified provisions.
useCasesLawyers.realWorld.courtFiling.ti: Court Filing Analysis: Arguments and Precedents
useCasesLawyers.realWorld.courtFiling.p1: A litigator receives opposing counsel's 30-page motion for summary judgment and needs to understand the key arguments quickly. They upload the filing and ask: "Summarize the main arguments for summary judgment." DocTalk returns a structured summary, with each argument cited to specific paragraphs in the motion.
useCasesLawyers.realWorld.courtFiling.p2: Follow-up questions refine the analysis: "What cases does the motion cite for the standard of review?" surfaces the precedent citations. "What facts does opposing counsel rely on for the absence of genuine dispute?" pinpoints the factual arguments. Each answer points to the specific page, allowing the litigator to read the original language in context before drafting a response.
useCasesLawyers.realWorld.patentReview.t: Patent Review: Prior Art and Claims Analysis
useCasesLawyers.realWorld.patentReview.p: A patent attorney reviewing a competitor's patent application uploads the document and asks: "What are the independent claims?" DocTalk extracts the claims with citations to their location in the patent. Follow-up questions like "What prior art is referenced?" and "How does claim 1 differ from the cited prior art?" help the attorney quickly assess the patent's scope and validity. The citations link directly to the relevant sections of the patent document, making it efficient to cross-reference claims against the specification.
useCasesLawyers.whyCitations.title: Why Citations Are Critical for Legal Work
useCasesLawyers.whyCitations.p1: In legal practice, every assertion must be traceable to a source. You cannot tell a client that "the contract contains an indemnification clause" without pointing to the specific section. You cannot brief a judge on opposing counsel's arguments without citing the exact paragraphs. Accuracy is not optional; it is a professional obligation.
useCasesLawyers.whyCitations.p2: General-purpose AI chatbots pose a real risk for legal professionals. They generate answers from training data and may fabricate contract terms, invent case citations, or misstate legal standards. Lawyers have already faced sanctions for relying on AI-generated citations that turned out to be fictitious.
useCasesLawyers.whyCitations.p3post: addresses this directly. Every AI answer includes numbered citations that link to specific passages in your uploaded document. Click a citation, and the document viewer scrolls to the exact text and highlights it. The AI cannot fabricate what is not in your document, because its answers are grounded in the actual text through Retrieval-Augmented Generation.
useCasesLawyers.whyCitations.p4: This makes DocTalk a document navigation tool, not an oracle. It helps you find relevant provisions faster and understand complex documents more quickly. The legal analysis, judgment, and advice remain yours.

useCasesLawyers.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
useCasesLawyers.faq.q1.question: Is DocTalk secure for confidential legal documents?
useCasesLawyers.faq.q1.answer: Yes. DocTalk encrypts all uploaded documents with AES-256 encryption (SSE-S3) at rest. Your documents are never used for AI model training. DocTalk is GDPR-compliant and provides data export functionality for compliance requirements.
useCasesLawyers.faq.q2.question: Can it analyze contracts?
useCasesLawyers.faq.q2.answer: Yes. DocTalk can analyze contracts in PDF, DOCX, and other formats. You can ask questions like "Find all indemnification clauses," "What are the termination conditions?", or "Summarize the liability provisions." Each answer includes numbered citations that link to the exact clause.
useCasesLawyers.faq.q3.question: How accurate is AI for legal analysis?
useCasesLawyers.faq.q3.answer: DocTalk uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to base every answer on the actual text of your document. Every answer includes numbered citations so you can verify each claim. DocTalk is a research acceleration tool, not a substitute for legal judgment.
useCasesLawyers.faq.q4.question: Does it work with scanned PDFs?
useCasesLawyers.faq.q4.answer: DocTalk works best with text-based PDFs where the text layer is preserved. Most modern legal documents produce text-based PDFs. Scanned image-only PDFs without OCR may have limited text extraction.
useCasesLawyers.faq.q5.question: Is there a team plan?
useCasesLawyers.faq.q5.answer: DocTalk currently offers individual plans: Free (500 credits/month), Plus ($9.99/month for 3,000 credits), and Pro ($19.99/month for 9,000 credits). Team and enterprise plans are on the roadmap.

featuresMultilingual.howItWorks.p1: DocTalk 的多语言支持远超简单翻译。驱动 DocTalk 的 AI 模型——DeepSeek、Mistral Medium 和 Mistral Large——原生支持多语言，在数十种语言的文本上训练。它们不会将您的问题翻译成英语、处理后再翻译回来。它们直接理解您的语言。
featuresMultilingual.howItWorks.p2: 当您上传文档时，DocTalk 以原始语言提取文本并为语义搜索建立索引。当您提问时，语义搜索跨语言运行，即使您用与文档不同的语言提问也能找到相关段落。然后 AI 以您偏好的语言生成答案，引用指向原文。
featuresMultilingual.howItWorks.p3: 整个 DocTalk 界面——按钮、菜单、标签、错误消息、账单页面——均在所有 11 种语言中完全本地化。随时使用页头的语言选择器切换语言，整个应用即时更新。

featuresMultilingual.faq.title: 常见问题

featuresMultilingual.faq.a1: 是的。DocTalk 支持跨语言分析。上传一种语言的文档，用另一种语言提问。AI 会跨语言找到相关段落，并用您提问的语言回答。
featuresMultilingual.faq.q2: DocTalk 支持中日韩 PDF 吗？
featuresMultilingual.faq.a2: 是的。DocTalk 包含完整的 CJK 支持，使用正确的 CMap 和标准字体渲染。无论 PDF 编码如何，字符都能正确显示。引用高亮和语义搜索原生支持 CJK 文本。
featuresMultilingual.faq.a3: 整个 DocTalk 界面支持 11 种语言：英语、简体中文、日语、西班牙语、德语、法语、韩语、葡萄牙语、意大利语、阿拉伯语和印地语。随时从语言选择器切换。
featuresMultilingual.faq.q4: 免费方案支持多语言对话吗？
featuresMultilingual.faq.a4: 是的。所有 11 种语言在每个方案上都可用，包括每月 500 额度的免费版。没有任何方案有语言限制。
featuresMultilingual.cta.title: 用您的语言与文档对话
featuresMultilingual.cta.subtitle: 试用免费演示体验多语言文档对话。上传任何语言的文档并获取带来源引用的 AI 答案。
featuresPerformance.hero.title: 选择您的 AI 性能模式
featuresPerformance.hero.subtitle: 并非每个问题都需要相同的深度。DocTalk 提供三种 AI 性能模式，让您为每个问题平衡速度、质量和成本。随时切换模式，甚至在对话中途。
featuresPerformance.modes.title: 三种模式，三种用途
featuresPerformance.mode.quick.name: 快速
featuresPerformance.mode.quick.descripti: 优化速度和效率。几秒内获得简洁的答案。适合事实查找、简单总结和快速检查。
featuresPerformance.mode.quick.bestFor3: 有明确答案的是/否问题
featuresPerformance.mode.balanced.name: 均衡
featuresPerformance.mode.balanced.descri: 日常工作的主力。均衡模式提供详细、结构良好的答案，涵盖细微差别和上下文。适合大多数文档分析任务。
featuresPerformance.mode.balanced.bestFo: 关于文档内容的一般问答
featuresPerformance.mode.thorough.name: 深度
featuresPerformance.mode.thorough.descri: 最大深度和推理能力。深度模式使用最强大的模型处理需要从整个文档中综合信息的复杂多部分问题。适合专业分析。
featuresPerformance.mode.thorough.availa: 仅限 Plus 和 Pro 方案
featuresPerformance.whenToUse.title: 何时使用每种模式
featuresPerformance.whenToUse.quick: 是简单问题的默认选择。需要查找特定日期、定义或数据点？快速模式几秒内找到，仅需 2 额度。在您扫描文档寻找特定信息时，速度足够快速。
featuresPerformance.whenToUse.balanced: 是大多数人大多数时候使用的模式。当您需要详细解释、带上下文的总结，或连接文档多个部分的答案时，均衡模式以每个问题 8 额度的价格提供。它提供深度阅读的深度而无需高级成本。
featuresPerformance.whenToUse.thorough: 用于高风险分析。复杂的法律问题、多因素财务分析，或需要 AI 仔细推理文档多个部分的学术研究。以 24 额度的价格，使用最强大的模型提供全面、细致的答案。在 Plus 和 Pro 方案上可用。
featuresPerformance.whenToUse.switching: 您可以在同一对话中在问题之间切换模式。从快速开始了解概况，然后在遇到复杂主题时切换到均衡或深度。每个问题根据使用的模式独立计费。
featuresPerformance.faq.title: 常见问题
featuresPerformance.faq.a1: 快速（2 额度）使用 DeepSeek V3.2 提供快速简洁的答案。均衡（8 额度）使用 Mistral Medium 3.1 进行详细的日常分析。深度（24 额度）使用 Mistral Large 2512 进行深入复杂推理。每种模式使用针对其用途优化的不同 AI 模型。
featuresPerformance.faq.q2: 对话过程中可以切换模式吗？
featuresPerformance.faq.a2: 是的。页头中的模式选择器可随时切换。每条消息根据该特定问题使用的模式计费，所以可以自由混合搭配。
featuresPerformance.faq.q3: 免费方案可以使用深度模式吗？
featuresPerformance.faq.a3: 不可以。深度模式需要 Plus（$9.99/月）或 Pro（$19.99/月）订阅。免费账户可使用快速和均衡模式，每月 500 额度。
featuresPerformance.faq.a4: 简单查找和事实问题使用快速。一般问答和解释使用均衡。复杂分析、多部分问题和需要最高准确度的专业工作使用深度。
featuresPerformance.cta.title: 试用全部三种模式
featuresPerformance.cta.subtitle: 免费演示使用快速模式。免费注册可获得均衡模式和每月 500 额度，或升级到 Plus 解锁深度模式。
featuresDemo.hero.title: 免费试用 DocTalk——无需账户
featuresDemo.hero.subtitle: 即刻与 AI 聊天了解示例文档。无需账户、无需信用卡、无需邮箱。使用 3 个预备好的演示文档查看引用高亮、多格式支持和性能模式。
featuresDemo.instant.title: 即时演示——无需设置
featuresDemo.instant.subtitle: 三个示例文档已预加载，可以立即对话。点击任何文档、输入问题，几秒内即可看到 DocTalk 的工作方式。
featuresDemo.docs.doc1.title: 示例 PDF
featuresDemo.docs.doc1.description: 一份真实的 PDF 文档，可进行 AI 分析。提问并查看带页面级导航的引用高亮。
featuresDemo.docs.doc2.title: 示例报告
featuresDemo.docs.doc2.description: 探索 DocTalk 如何处理章节、标题和数据等结构化内容——全程带引用答案。
featuresDemo.docs.doc3.title: 示例文档
featuresDemo.docs.doc3.description: 第三个演示文件，可尝试不同的提问风格，看 AI 如何跨格式提取信息。
featuresDemo.whatYouGet.title: 演示包含什么
featuresDemo.whatYouGet.item1.label: 每次会话 5 条消息
featuresDemo.whatYouGet.item1.descriptio: 足够探索文档和测试引用高亮。
featuresDemo.whatYouGet.item2.label: 引用高亮
featuresDemo.whatYouGet.item2.descriptio: 点击任意编号引用跳转到源文本。
featuresDemo.whatYouGet.item3.label: AI 驱动的答案
featuresDemo.whatYouGet.item3.descriptio: 演示模式使用 DeepSeek V3.2 提供快速响应。
featuresDemo.whatYouGet.item4.label: 完整功能预览
featuresDemo.whatYouGet.item4.descriptio: 查看文档查看器、对话面板和引用导航。
featuresDemo.compare.title: 演示 vs 免费方案 vs 付费方案
featuresDemo.compare.subtitle: 演示是预览。免费账户解锁您自己的上传。付费方案解锁更多额度和功能。
featuresDemo.steps.title: 如何开始
featuresDemo.steps.step1.title: 点击演示链接
featuresDemo.steps.step1.description: 前往演示页面——无需账户、无需邮箱，无需填写任何内容。
featuresDemo.steps.step2.title: 选择文档
featuresDemo.steps.step2.description: 从 3 个预加载的示例文档中选择一个进行探索。
featuresDemo.steps.step3.title: 提出问题
featuresDemo.steps.step3.description: 在对话面板中输入关于文档的任何问题。
featuresDemo.steps.step4.title: 点击引用
featuresDemo.steps.step4.description: 点击任意编号引用跳转到高亮的源文本。
featuresDemo.faq.title: 常见问题
featuresDemo.faq.a1: 是的。演示完全免费。无需信用卡、无需账户、无需邮箱。点击即可开始与 AI 聊天示例文档。如果您想上传自己的文档，免费方案提供每月 500 额度——同样无需信用卡。
featuresDemo.faq.a2: 演示不需要。演示无需注册即可使用。要上传您自己的文档并保存聊天记录，您需要一个免费账户，可通过 Google、Microsoft 或邮箱创建。
featuresDemo.faq.a3: 不会自动发生任何事。您可以随意使用演示。准备上传您自己的文档时，创建免费账户（500 额度/月）或升级到 Plus（$9.99/月，3,000 额度）或 Pro（$19.99/月，9,000 额度）。
featuresDemo.faq.q4: 可以免费上传自己的文档吗？
featuresDemo.faq.a4: 是的。创建免费账户（无需信用卡），可上传最多 3 个文档（每个 25MB），每月 500 额度。使用快速模式足够提问数十个问题。
featuresDemo.faq.a5: 演示不使用额度。拥有账户后，免费方案包含每月 500 额度。快速模式每个问题 2 额度，均衡 8 额度，深度 24 额度。Plus（$9.99）提供 3,000 额度，Pro（$19.99）提供 9,000 额度。
featuresDemo.cta.title: 准备好试试了吗？
featuresDemo.cta.subtitle: 无需注册。无需信用卡。点击即可开始与 AI 聊天真实文档。
featuresHub.heroSubtitle: 上传任何文档，用自然语言提问，获取带来源引用的 AI 驱动答案。探索让 DocTalk 成为最透明的 AI 文档工具的功能。
featuresHub.citationsDesc: 每个 AI 回答都包含编号引用。点击任意引用可跳转到文档中高亮显示的确切源文本。
featuresHub.multiFormatDesc: 上传 PDF、DOCX、PPTX、XLSX、TXT、Markdown 或任意 URL。使用 AI 与任何文档格式对话。
featuresHub.multilingualDesc: 使用英语、中文、日语、西班牙语、德语、法语、韩语、葡萄牙语、意大利语、阿拉伯语和印地语与文档对话。
featuresHub.freeDemoDesc: 即刻体验 AI 文档对话。无需注册、无需信用卡。3 个示例文档随时可用。
featuresHub.performanceModesDesc: 快速模式用于快速回答，均衡模式用于日常使用，深度模式用于深入分析。选择合适的速度和深度。
featuresHub.workflowsTitle: 将功能与实际工作流配对
featuresHub.workflowsDesc: 当您将产品功能与实际工作结合时，DocTalk 效果最好：研究、法律审查、财务分析或工具评估。
featuresHub.ctaText: 通过免费演示查看所有功能——无需账户。
featuresCitations.heroTitle: 可信赖的 AI 答案：带实时高亮的来源引用
featuresCitations.heroSubtitle: DocTalk 生成的每个答案都包含编号引用，直接链接到文档中的原文。点击任意引用即可滚动到来源并查看高亮。不再猜测 AI 是否正确。
featuresCitations.howSubtitle: 从提问到验证答案只需三个简单步骤，整个过程仅需几秒。
featuresCitations.howStep1Desc: 在对话面板中输入任何问题。DocTalk 使用语义搜索（RAG）从整个文档中找到最相关的段落，无论文档是 5 页还是 500 页。
featuresCitations.howStep2Title: AI 生成带编号引用的答案
featuresCitations.howStep2Desc: AI 用清晰的自然语言回答。在回复中，您会看到编号标记如 [1]、[2]、[3]——每个都指向从文档中提取的特定段落。
featuresCitations.howStep3Title: 点击引用查看高亮来源
featuresCitations.howStep3Desc: 点击引用编号，文档查看器会立即滚动到确切的源文本。段落以黄色高亮显示，您可以阅读原始上下文并自行验证 AI 的说法。
featuresCitations.whyPara1: AI 幻觉是 AI 文档分析中最大的未解决问题。语言模型可以生成流畅、自信且听起来完全合理的答案——但完全是捏造的。研究表明，即使是最先进的语言模型也有 3% 到 27% 的时间产生幻觉，具体取决于任务。
featuresCitations.whyPara2: 对于处理法律合同、财务报告、学术论文或医学文档的专业人士来说，一个虚构的事实可能导致代价高昂的错误。标准 AI 聊天机器人回答没有来源引用，让您无法区分正确答案和幻觉。
featuresCitations.whyPara3: DocTalk 通过引用优先的方法解决了这个问题。每个答案都带有编号的来源引用。每个引用都可点击。每次点击都会带您到原始文档中的确切段落并高亮显示，让您在上下文中阅读。AI 不仅告诉您答案——还向您展示答案的来源。
featuresCitations.whyPara4: 这将 AI 从黑盒转变为透明的研究助手。您获得 AI 的速度和人工研究的可验证性。您可以随时检查每个说法。
featuresCitations.layersSubtitle: DocTalk 不只是附加页码。它从三个独立的文档理解层构建引用。
featuresCitations.layer1Title: 第一层：来源提取（RAG 检索）
featuresCitations.layer1Desc: 当您提问时，DocTalk 使用检索增强生成对整个文档进行语义搜索。它通过理解含义而非关键词匹配来找到最相关的段落。AI 仅看到这些检索到的段落，使其答案扎根于您的实际文档文本，而非通用知识。
featuresCitations.layer2Desc: 每个检索到的段落都携带关于其位置的元数据：页码、章节标题和在文档中的位置。当 AI 生成引用时，会附上位置数据，让您准确知道信息来自文档的哪个位置。
featuresCitations.layer3Title: 第三层：可视化高亮导航
featuresCitations.layer3Desc: 最后一层是交互式高亮体验。点击任意引用，文档查看器会滚动到源段落。对于 PDF，使用边界框坐标精确高亮页面上的文本区域。对于 DOCX、PPTX 和其他文本格式，使用渐进式文本片段匹配来定位和高亮段落。
featuresCitations.compSubtitle: 并非所有 AI 文档工具处理引用的方式都相同。以下是 DocTalk 的对比。
featuresCitations.compDisclaimer: 对比基于公开可用的功能信息。“部分支持”表示该功能以有限形式存在。
featuresCitations.useCasesSubtitle: 引用高亮不是锦上添花。对这些专业人士来说，它是必不可少的。
featuresCitations.useCaseAcademicDesc: 在论文中引用前验证 AI 提取的说法。点击跳转到原始段落以确认确切措辞、检查上下文并确保准确归因。绝不引用 AI 幻觉的内容。
featuresCitations.useCaseLegalDesc: 导航到确切的合同条款、特定的政策段落和精确的法规语言。当客户询问特定条款时，点击引用以在完整上下文中阅读原始条款。
featuresCitations.useCaseFinanceDesc: 引用年报、财报电话会议和财务报告中的具体数据、脚注和数据点。点击任意引用跳转到 AI 引用的确切表格、图表或段落。
featuresCitations.faq1A: DocTalk 使用 RAG（检索增强生成）在生成答案前找到最相关的段落。每个引用指向从文档中提取的特定段落。准确度取决于文档质量和问题的具体性，但您始终可以点击任何引用来自行验证。目标是完全透明：您看到的正是 AI 看到的。
featuresCitations.faq2A: 是的。AI 答案中的每个编号引用都可点击。点击后文档查看器会滚动到确切的源段落并以黄色高亮显示，让您在上下文中阅读原文。对于 PDF，这会高亮页面上的边界框区域。对于其他格式，使用文本片段匹配。
featuresCitations.faq3Q: 支持 DOCX 和 PPTX 吗？
featuresCitations.faq3A: 是的。引用高亮适用于所有 7 种支持格式：PDF、DOCX、PPTX、XLSX、TXT、Markdown 和 URL。引用导航会适配每种格式——PDF 使用边界框坐标，文本格式使用渐进式文本片段匹配。
featuresCitations.faq4A: DocTalk 使用 RAG 检索将每个答案扎根于您的实际文档文本。AI 仅看到文档中的相关段落，而非通用知识。引用让您可以验证每个说法。如果信息不在文档中，DocTalk 会告诉您，而不是编造答案。
featuresCitations.faq5Q: 免费方案有引用功能吗？
featuresCitations.faq5A: 是的。引用高亮在所有方案中可用，包括每月 500 额度的免费版。您也可以在免费演示中即刻体验，无需创建账户。
featuresCitations.ctaSubtitle: 试用免费演示亲自体验引用高亮。上传文档或与我们的示例文件对话。无需账户。
featuresMultiFormat.heroTitle: 使用 AI 与任何文档格式对话
featuresMultiFormat.heroSubtitle: 上传 PDF、Word、PowerPoint、Excel、文本文件、Markdown 或粘贴任意 URL。DocTalk 解析您的文档，理解其结构，让您提问并获得带引用的答案——无论格式如何。
featuresMultiFormat.formatsSubtitle: DocTalk 不只是打开文件——它理解结构、智能提取内容并保留上下文，实现准确的 AI 分析。
featuresMultiFormat.formatPdfDesc: 完整的文本提取，带页面级引用和边界框高亮。支持扫描文档、复杂布局和 CJK 字符。
featuresMultiFormat.formatDocxDesc: 段落和表格提取，采用交错的正文元素迭代。保留标题结构和表格格式以实现准确的 AI 分析。
featuresMultiFormat.formatPptxDesc: 幻灯片内容和演讲者备注提取，带幻灯片级引用。直接导航到 AI 引用的幻灯片。
featuresMultiFormat.formatXlsxDesc: 表格数据和单元格值提取，带工作表导航。询问电子表格数据并获得引用特定单元格的答案。
featuresMultiFormat.formatTxtDesc: 纯文本和 Markdown 渲染，完整格式支持。Markdown 表格、代码块和标题均被保留。
featuresMultiFormat.formatUrlDesc: 网页内容提取和分析。粘贴任何 URL，使用与其他文档相同的引用和高亮功能与页面内容对话。
featuresMultiFormat.whyTitle: 为什么多格式支持很重要
featuresMultiFormat.whyPara1: 大多数 AI 文档工具仅支持 PDF。但您的实际工作流远不止 PDF。您会收到同事的 Word 文档、审查客户的 PowerPoint 演示文稿、分析 Excel 电子表格中的数据，以及阅读想要日后参考的网络文章。
featuresMultiFormat.whyPara2: 使用仅支持 PDF 的工具，您被迫先将每个文档转换为 PDF——丢失格式、破坏表格并增加摩擦。使用 DocTalk，您上传原始文件即可立即开始对话。
featuresMultiFormat.whyPara3: 每种格式都有专用的解析器。DOCX 文件保留交错的段落和表格结构。PPTX 文件提取幻灯片内容和演讲者备注。XLSX 文件维护单元格关系和工作表组织。这种结构化提取意味着 AI 以您理解文档的方式来理解它，而不是作为一堵平坦的文字墙。
featuresMultiFormat.howStep1Desc: 拖放任何支持的文件或粘贴 URL。DocTalk 自动检测格式并选择正确的解析器。无需手动转换。
featuresMultiFormat.howStep2Desc: 文档使用格式特定的提取器异步解析。文本、表格、标题和元数据被提取并索引以进行语义搜索。
featuresMultiFormat.howStep3Desc: 在对话面板中提出任何问题。AI 搜索提取的内容，生成带编号引用的答案，让您点击验证每一个。
featuresMultiFormat.detailPdfText: 边界框引用高亮精确定位每页上的确切文本区域。完整的 CJK（中日韩）字符支持，使用正确的 CMap 渲染。
featuresMultiFormat.detailDocxText: 正文元素迭代按自然阅读顺序提取段落和表格——而非作为单独的列表。标题层次结构被保留以提供上下文。
featuresMultiFormat.detailPptxText: 演讲者备注与幻灯片内容一起提取，让 AI 可以访问幻灯片上不可见的演示者解释和上下文。
featuresMultiFormat.detailXlsxText: 表格数据连同单元格引用一起提取。AI 可以回答关于特定数据范围的问题并引用源单元格。
featuresMultiFormat.detailTxtText: Markdown 以完整格式渲染：表格（remark-gfm）、代码块、标题和列表。纯文本文件保留空白格式显示。
featuresMultiFormat.detailUrlText: 网页内容被提取和清理，移除导航、广告和样板内容。文章内容可用于与其他文档相同引用功能的 AI 对话。
featuresMultiFormat.compTitle: 与仅支持 PDF 的工具对比

featuresMultiFormat.faq1Q: 可以上传 DOCX 文件吗？
featuresMultiFormat.faq1A: 是的。DocTalk 完全支持 Microsoft Word（.docx）文件。它提取段落、表格和标题结构，同时保留文档布局以实现准确的 AI 分析。引用链接回文档的特定部分。
featuresMultiFormat.faq2Q: DocTalk 能读取 PowerPoint 幻灯片吗？
featuresMultiFormat.faq2A: 是的。上传任何 .pptx 文件，DocTalk 会提取幻灯片内容和演讲者备注。引用引用特定幻灯片，您可以直接导航到来源。幻灯片级引用与其他格式使用相同的点击高亮功能。
featuresMultiFormat.faq3Q: 可以分析 Excel 电子表格吗？
featuresMultiFormat.faq3A: 是的。DocTalk 解析 .xlsx 文件，跨工作表提取表格数据和单元格值。询问您的数据——汇总列、比较行、查找特定值——并获得引用源单元格的答案。
featuresMultiFormat.faq4A: 是的。粘贴任何 URL，DocTalk 会自动提取网页内容。提取的文本可用于与任何上传文档相同引用高亮功能的 AI 对话。
featuresMultiFormat.faq5A: 文件大小限制取决于您的方案：免费账户可上传最大 25MB（3 个文档），Plus 最大 50MB（20 个文档），Pro 最大 100MB（无限文档）。所有方案支持最多 500 页的文档。

compareChatpdf.heroDescription: Both DocTalk and ChatPDF let you chat with documents using AI, but they take different approaches. ChatPDF focuses exclusively on PDFs with a simple interface, while DocTalk supports seven document formats and provides real-time citation highlighting so you can verify every answer against the original source. Here is how they compare across features, pricing, and performance.
compareChatpdf.author: DocTalk Team
compareChatpdf.table.doctalk.supportedFo: 7 (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, MD, URL)
compareChatpdf.whatIsDocTalkDescription: DocTalk is an AI-powered document question-and-answer platform that supports seven file formats: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, Markdown, and web URLs. Upload any document, ask questions in natural language, and receive answers with real-time citation highlighting that scrolls to and visually highlights the exact source passage in your original document. The interface supports 11 languages, offers three AI performance modes (Quick, Balanced, and Thorough), and includes a free demo that requires no account creation. DocTalk is designed for students, researchers, legal professionals, and anyone who needs to extract information from documents quickly while maintaining full traceability to the source material.
compareChatpdf.whatIsChatPDFDescription: ChatPDF is one of the earliest AI PDF chat tools, launched in early 2023. It allows users to upload PDF files and ask questions about their contents using a conversational interface. The tool references page numbers in its answers, making it possible to locate information manually. ChatPDF has built a large user base thanks to its simplicity and early-mover advantage. The free tier allows 2 PDFs per day with 3 questions each, while the Plus plan ($19.99/month) removes these limits. ChatPDF focuses solely on PDF files and does not support other document formats like Word, PowerPoint, or Excel.
compareChatpdf.feature.formatSupportP1: This is the most significant difference between the two tools. ChatPDF supports only PDF files. If you need to analyze a Word document, a PowerPoint presentation, an Excel spreadsheet, or a web page, you must first convert it to PDF before uploading, which is inconvenient and can introduce formatting issues.
compareChatpdf.feature.formatSupportP2Pr: DocTalk natively supports seven formats: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, Markdown, and web URLs. Each format is parsed with a dedicated extractor that preserves the document structure, including tables, headings, and paragraph breaks. For example, the XLSX parser preserves spreadsheet structure, and the PPTX parser maintains slide order with speaker notes. This means you can upload your files directly without any conversion step, and the AI receives clean, structured text that produces better answers. See our
compareChatpdf.feature.citationsP1: Both tools use retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to answer questions based on your document content. ChatPDF provides page number references in its responses, which helps you know roughly where to look. However, you need to manually scroll to the referenced page and search for the relevant passage yourself.
compareChatpdf.feature.citationsP2Pre: DocTalk takes citations significantly further with real-time citation highlighting. When the AI answers your question, each citation is a clickable link. Clicking it instantly scrolls the document viewer to the exact passage and visually highlights it, allowing you to verify the answer in seconds without any manual searching. This feature is particularly valuable for academic research, legal document review, and any task where accuracy matters. Learn more about our
compareChatpdf.feature.languagesP1: ChatPDF has a primarily English interface with limited multilingual capability. While the underlying AI models can process text in multiple languages, the interface, help documentation, and user experience are centered around English speakers.
compareChatpdf.feature.languagesP2Pre: DocTalk provides a fully localized interface in 11 languages: English, Chinese, Spanish, Japanese, German, French, Korean, Portuguese, Italian, Arabic, and Hindi. Every element of the interface, from buttons and labels to error messages and billing pages, is translated. The AI can also respond in the same language as your question, regardless of the document language. This makes DocTalk a strong choice for multilingual teams and non-English speakers. See our
compareChatpdf.feature.pricingP1: ChatPDF offers a free tier limited to 2 PDFs per day with 3 questions each and a maximum file size of 10 MB. The Plus plan costs $19.99 per month and includes 50 PDFs per day, 50 questions per PDF, and a 32 MB file size limit.
compareChatpdf.feature.pricingP2Pre: DocTalk takes a different approach. First, there is an
compareChatpdf.feature.pricingP2Mid: that requires absolutely no signup, so you can try the product before creating an account. The free tier includes 500 credits per month. The Plus plan ($9.99/month) includes 3,000 credits and unlocks Thorough mode and export features. The Pro plan ($19.99/month) adds 9,000 credits and custom instructions. DocTalk is generally more affordable: the Plus plan at $9.99 offers comparable or better value than ChatPDF Plus at $19.99. View
compareChatpdf.feature.performanceP1: ChatPDF uses a single AI model for all users and generally provides responses within a few seconds. The simplicity of its architecture, focused solely on PDF, means parsing is straightforward.
compareChatpdf.feature.performanceP2: DocTalk offers three performance modes. Quick mode (powered by DeepSeek V3.2) delivers fast responses ideal for simple questions. Balanced mode (Mistral Medium) provides higher accuracy for nuanced questions. Thorough mode (Mistral Large) gives the most detailed analysis for complex research tasks. This flexibility lets you choose the right speed-quality tradeoff for each question. Document parsing uses dedicated Celery workers that process files asynchronously, so uploading and parsing happens in the background while you can start reviewing the document.
compareChatpdf.feature.securityP1: ChatPDF states that documents are stored securely and can be deleted by the user. The privacy policy covers basic data handling practices.
compareChatpdf.feature.securityP2: DocTalk implements SSE-S3 encryption for all uploaded documents, SSRF protection for URL ingestion, magic-byte file validation to prevent malicious uploads, and structured security event logging. Documents are never used to train AI models. DocTalk also provides a GDPR data export endpoint and cookie consent management. The OAuth implementation strips tokens after linking, and the Docker deployment runs as a non-root user for defense in depth.
compareChatpdf.whoDocTalk.item1: Users who work with multiple document formats (Word, PowerPoint, Excel) and want to avoid converting everything to PDF
compareChatpdf.whoDocTalk.item2: Researchers and legal professionals who need verifiable citations with one-click source highlighting
compareChatpdf.whoDocTalk.item3: Non-English speakers who need a fully localized interface in their language
compareChatpdf.whoDocTalk.item4: Budget-conscious users who want premium features at a lower price point ($9.99 vs $19.99)
compareChatpdf.whoDocTalk.item5: Users who want to try before committing, using the no-signup instant demo
compareChatpdf.whoChatPDF.item1: Users who exclusively work with PDF files and do not need other format support
compareChatpdf.whoChatPDF.item2: People who prefer an established tool with a large community and proven track record
compareChatpdf.whoChatPDF.item3: Users who need the simplest possible interface with minimal learning curve
compareChatpdf.whoChatPDF.item4: Casual users who only need to ask a few questions occasionally (2 free PDFs per day may suffice)
compareChatpdf.verdictP1: ChatPDF pioneered the AI PDF chat category and remains a solid choice for users who only work with PDF files and want a simple, proven interface. Its large user base and early-mover advantage give it brand recognition.
compareChatpdf.verdictP2: However, DocTalk offers a more comprehensive solution. The support for seven document formats eliminates the need for file conversion. Real-time citation highlighting provides a level of answer verification that ChatPDF does not match. The 11-language interface makes DocTalk accessible to a global audience. And at $9.99 per month for the Plus plan versus ChatPDF Plus at $19.99, DocTalk delivers more features for less money.
compareChatpdf.verdictP3Pre: For most users, especially those who work with mixed document formats or need citation verification, DocTalk is the stronger choice. The best way to decide is to
compareChatpdf.verdictP3Post: and see the citation highlighting in action.
compareChatpdf.faq.a1: DocTalk supports 7 document formats (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, MD, URL) while ChatPDF only supports PDF. DocTalk also provides real-time citation highlighting that shows you exactly where in the document each answer comes from, a feature ChatPDF lacks. However, ChatPDF has been around longer and has a larger user base.
compareChatpdf.faq.a2: ChatPDF mentions page numbers in its answers but does not offer inline citation highlighting. DocTalk provides real-time citation highlighting that visually highlights the exact passage in your document when you click a citation, making it much easier to verify AI answers.
compareChatpdf.faq.q3: Can I use DocTalk for free?
compareChatpdf.faq.a3: Yes. DocTalk offers an instant demo that requires no signup at all, plus a free tier with 500 credits per month after you create an account. ChatPDF offers a free tier limited to 2 PDFs per day with 3 questions each.
compareChatpdf.faq.a4: DocTalk supports 11 interface languages (English, Chinese, Spanish, Japanese, German, French, Korean, Portuguese, Italian, Arabic, and Hindi) and can analyze documents in any language. ChatPDF is primarily English-focused with limited multilingual support.
compareChatpdf.faq.q5: Can DocTalk handle Word and PowerPoint files?
compareChatpdf.faq.a5: Yes. DocTalk natively supports PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, Markdown, and web URLs. ChatPDF only processes PDF files, so you would need to convert other formats to PDF first.
compareChatpdf.ctaTitle: Try DocTalk Free — No Signup Required
compareChatpdf.ctaDescription: Upload a document and see citation highlighting in action. No account needed.
compareNotebooklm.heroTitle: DocTalk vs NotebookLM: Which AI Document Tool?
compareNotebooklm.heroDescription: Google NotebookLM and DocTalk represent two different philosophies in AI document analysis. NotebookLM is a free, Google-integrated tool built around multi-source notebooks with a unique audio podcast feature. DocTalk is an independent, privacy-first platform focused on deep single-document analysis with real-time citation highlighting across seven formats and eleven languages. Here is how they compare in detail.
compareNotebooklm.author: DocTalk Team
compareNotebooklm.table.doctalk.supporte: 7 (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, MD, URL)
compareNotebooklm.table.competitor.suppo: PDF, Google Docs, web URLs, text, YouTube
compareNotebooklm.whatIsDocTalkDescripti: DocTalk is an independent AI document Q&A platform designed for deep, verifiable analysis of individual documents. It supports seven file formats (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, Markdown, and web URLs) and provides real-time citation highlighting: click any citation in an AI answer to instantly scroll to and highlight the exact source passage in a side-by-side document viewer. The interface is fully localized in 11 languages. Three AI performance modes (Quick, Balanced, Thorough) let you choose between speed and depth. DocTalk requires no vendor lock-in. You can sign in with Google, Microsoft, or email, and your data is encrypted and never used for AI training.
compareNotebooklm.whatIsNotebookLMDescri: Google NotebookLM (formerly Project Tailwind) is a free AI-powered research tool that lets you create notebooks with multiple source documents. You can upload PDFs, link Google Docs, paste text, and add web URLs or YouTube videos as sources. The AI synthesizes information across all sources in a notebook, making it useful for literature reviews and research projects. NotebookLM is unique in offering AI-generated audio podcasts that summarize your sources as a natural-sounding conversation. It runs on Google Gemini and is deeply integrated with the Google ecosystem. A Google account is required.
compareNotebooklm.feature.formatSupportP: NotebookLM supports PDF files, Google Docs (natively via Drive), web URLs, pasted text, and YouTube video transcripts. It does not natively support DOCX, PPTX, or XLSX files, though you can upload Word documents to Google Drive and convert them. The YouTube integration is a unique strength for video-based research.

DocTalk directly accepts PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, Markdown, and web URLs with dedicated parsers for each format. The XLSX parser preserves spreadsheet structure, and the PPTX parser maintains slide order with speaker notes. No conversion step is needed. While DocTalk does not support YouTube or Google Docs integration, it covers the most common business and academic document formats. See
compareNotebooklm.feature.citationsP1: NotebookLM provides inline citations numbered with source references. Clicking a citation highlights the relevant source in the notebook sidebar. The multi-source approach means answers can synthesize information from multiple documents, which is powerful for cross-referencing. The AI podcast feature creates engaging audio summaries, though it cannot replace detailed Q&A.
compareNotebooklm.feature.citationsP2Pre: DocTalk provides real-time citation highlighting in a dedicated side-by-side document viewer. Click any citation and the viewer scrolls to the exact passage with a visual highlight overlay. This is particularly effective for PDF documents where bounding-box coordinates enable precise highlighting. While DocTalk focuses on single-document analysis rather than multi-source synthesis, it provides deeper, more precise citations for individual documents. Learn more about
compareNotebooklm.feature.languagesP1: NotebookLM has a primarily English interface, though Gemini can process and respond in multiple languages. The audio podcast feature currently works best in English. The Google ecosystem provides some inherent multilingual support, but the NotebookLM interface itself is not fully localized.
compareNotebooklm.feature.languagesP2Pre: DocTalk offers a fully localized interface in 11 languages: English, Chinese, Spanish, Japanese, German, French, Korean, Portuguese, Italian, Arabic, and Hindi. Every interface element is translated, and the AI responds in the language of your question. This makes DocTalk the stronger choice for multilingual teams. See
compareNotebooklm.feature.pricingP1: NotebookLM is currently free. This is its biggest advantage. Google has not disclosed future pricing, but as with many Google products, it may remain free with usage limits while offering premium features at a cost. The tradeoff is that you are building your workflow inside Google ecosystem.
compareNotebooklm.feature.pricingP2Mid: with no signup required, plus 500 free credits per month. Paid plans start at $9.99/month (Plus) for 3,000 credits and go up to $19.99/month (Pro) for 9,000 credits with advanced features. While not free like NotebookLM, DocTalk provides transparent, predictable pricing without vendor lock-in. View
compareNotebooklm.feature.performanceP1: NotebookLM runs on Google Gemini and benefits from Google infrastructure. Responses are generally fast, though processing multiple large sources in a notebook can take time. The audio podcast generation is a longer process, typically taking a minute or more to produce.
compareNotebooklm.feature.performanceP2: DocTalk offers three performance modes. Quick mode (DeepSeek V3.2) provides the fastest responses. Balanced mode (Mistral Medium) is ideal for most questions. Thorough mode (Mistral Large) delivers the most comprehensive analysis. This flexibility is unavailable in NotebookLM, which is locked to Gemini.
compareNotebooklm.feature.securityP1: NotebookLM is subject to Google privacy policies. While Google states that NotebookLM does not use your data for training, it is still within the Google ecosystem, and your documents are stored on Google servers. This may be a concern for organizations with strict data governance requirements or those who prefer not to centralize data with a single tech company.
compareNotebooklm.feature.securityP2: DocTalk is an independent platform with SSE-S3 encryption, SSRF protection, and GDPR compliance features. Documents are never used for AI training. You can export all your data at any time, and the platform supports multiple authentication providers (Google, Microsoft, email) to avoid vendor lock-in. For organizations that need data sovereignty or want to avoid Google dependency, DocTalk is the more private choice.
compareNotebooklm.whoDocTalk.item1: Users who need precise citation highlighting to verify AI answers against source text
compareNotebooklm.whoDocTalk.item2: People working with DOCX, PPTX, or XLSX files that NotebookLM does not natively support
compareNotebooklm.whoDocTalk.item3: Non-English speakers who need a fully localized interface in their language
compareNotebooklm.whoDocTalk.item4: Organizations that need to avoid Google vendor lock-in or have strict data governance requirements
compareNotebooklm.whoDocTalk.item5: Users who want to choose between multiple AI models for different tasks
compareNotebooklm.whoNotebookLM.item1: Researchers who need to synthesize information across multiple documents simultaneously
compareNotebooklm.whoNotebookLM.item2: Users who want AI-generated audio podcasts summarizing their research
compareNotebooklm.whoNotebookLM.item3: People deeply embedded in the Google ecosystem (Drive, Docs, YouTube)
compareNotebooklm.whoNotebookLM.item4: Budget-conscious users who need a completely free tool
compareNotebooklm.whoNotebookLM.item5: Students working on literature reviews who need cross-document synthesis
compareNotebooklm.verdictP1: NotebookLM and DocTalk solve different problems. NotebookLM excels at multi-source research with its notebook paradigm and the unique audio podcast feature. Being free and backed by Google infrastructure makes it a compelling choice for students and researchers who are comfortable within the Google ecosystem.
compareNotebooklm.verdictP2: DocTalk excels at deep, verifiable single-document analysis. The real-time citation highlighting is in a class of its own for answer verification. Support for seven document formats (including DOCX, PPTX, and XLSX that NotebookLM lacks) and 11 interface languages makes it more versatile for professional and international use cases. The independence from any single tech platform is a real advantage for organizations concerned about vendor lock-in.
compareNotebooklm.verdictP3Pre: If you primarily need multi-source synthesis and love Google products, NotebookLM is excellent. If you need precise citation verification, work with business document formats, or want to avoid Google dependency,
compareNotebooklm.faq.a1: DocTalk and NotebookLM serve different purposes. DocTalk excels at single-document deep analysis with real-time citation highlighting, 7 format support, and 11 languages. NotebookLM is better for multi-source notebooks and offers unique AI-generated audio podcasts. DocTalk is the better choice if you need citation verification and format flexibility; NotebookLM is better for free multi-source research.
compareNotebooklm.faq.a2: Yes, Google NotebookLM is currently free to use, though it requires a Google account. Google has not yet announced pricing for future premium features. However, being free means you are subject to Google data practices and potential changes in service terms.
compareNotebooklm.faq.a3: NotebookLM shows inline citations that link to the source document within the notebook. However, it does not provide the real-time visual highlighting that DocTalk offers, where clicking a citation scrolls to and highlights the exact passage in a document viewer alongside the chat.
compareNotebooklm.faq.q4: Can I use DocTalk without a Google account?
compareNotebooklm.faq.a4: Yes. DocTalk supports Google OAuth, Microsoft OAuth, and email magic links for authentication. You can also use the instant demo with no account at all. NotebookLM requires a Google account, which may be a concern for users who prefer not to use Google services.
compareNotebooklm.faq.a5: DocTalk stores documents with SSE-S3 encryption, never trains AI on your data, and provides GDPR data export. NotebookLM is a Google product subject to Google privacy policies. DocTalk gives you more control and transparency over your data.
compareNotebooklm.ctaTitle: Try DocTalk Free — No Signup Required
compareNotebooklm.ctaDescription: No Google account needed. Upload a document and experience citation highlighting instantly.

altsHub.comparePrompt: Want a head-to-head comparison between two specific tools?
altsChatpdf.heroDescription: ChatPDF is a popular AI PDF chat tool, but it only supports PDF files, lacks citation highlighting, and its paid plan costs $19.99 per month. Whether you need multi-format support, better citations, team features, or a lower price, there are strong alternatives available. Here are the seven best ChatPDF alternatives in 2026, ranked by overall value for document analysis.
altsChatpdf.compareTitle: ChatPDF vs Top Alternative at a Glance
altsChatpdf.alt1Desc1: DocTalk is the most comprehensive ChatPDF alternative, addressing every major limitation of ChatPDF while adding features ChatPDF does not have. Where ChatPDF supports only PDF files, DocTalk handles seven formats: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, Markdown, and web URLs, with dedicated parsers for each format that preserve document structure including tables, headings, and slide layouts.
altsChatpdf.alt1Desc2: The standout feature is real-time citation highlighting. When the AI answers your question, every citation is a clickable link. Click it and the document viewer scrolls to the exact passage with a visual highlight overlay. This is a fundamental improvement over ChatPDF page number references, which require you to manually find the relevant text. For academic papers, legal documents, or any research where accuracy matters, this one feature alone justifies switching.
altsChatpdf.alt1Desc3Pre: DocTalk also offers three AI performance modes (Quick, Balanced, Thorough) instead of a single model, a fully localized interface in 11 languages, and a free demo that requires no signup at all. Paid plans start at $9.99 per month, which is half the price of ChatPDF Plus at $19.99. See our
altsChatpdf.adv2: Real-time citation highlighting with one-click verification
altsChatpdf.adv6: Half the price of ChatPDF Plus ($9.99 vs $19.99)
altsChatpdf.alt2Desc1: AskYourPDF stands out with its research-oriented ecosystem. The Chrome extension lets you analyze PDFs directly in your browser without uploading them separately. The Zotero plugin integrates directly with academic reference managers, making it easy to query your research library. An API is available for developers who want to build document AI into their own applications.
altsChatpdf.alt2Desc2Pre: The tradeoff is complexity. AskYourPDF has more moving parts than ChatPDF or DocTalk, which can be overwhelming for casual users. The interface is primarily English-only, and there is no real-time citation highlighting. Premium plans start at $14.99 per month. For researchers embedded in the Zotero ecosystem, AskYourPDF is hard to beat. For everyone else, simpler alternatives like DocTalk or ChatPDF may be better. See our
altsChatpdf.alt2BestFor: Academic researchers, Zotero users, developers needing API access
altsChatpdf.alt3Desc1: Humata is the only ChatPDF alternative on this list with robust team collaboration features. The Team plan ($49/user/month) includes shared workspaces, role-based access control, and the ability to share AI-generated insights across team members. Humata also uniquely supports video file analysis alongside PDFs and Word documents.
altsChatpdf.alt3Desc2: For individual users, Humata is less compelling. The free tier is limited to 60 pages per month, and the English-only interface is restrictive for international users. Page-based pricing means costs increase with document length rather than question count. But for teams needing collaborative document AI, Humata fills a niche that other tools do not.
altsChatpdf.alt3BestFor: Teams needing shared workspaces, organizations with video content
altsChatpdf.alt4Desc1: Google NotebookLM is the best completely free ChatPDF alternative. It supports multi-source notebooks where you can combine PDFs, Google Docs, web URLs, text, and YouTube transcripts into a single research workspace. The AI synthesizes information across all sources, which is perfect for literature reviews and cross-document research.
altsChatpdf.alt4Desc2: The unique AI-generated audio podcast feature creates natural-sounding conversational summaries of your sources, which is a genuinely novel way to consume research. The tradeoff is Google lock-in: you need a Google account, your data is on Google servers, and the English-focused interface limits international use. NotebookLM also does not support DOCX, PPTX, or XLSX natively.
altsChatpdf.alt4BestFor: Budget-conscious users, multi-source research, Google ecosystem users
altsChatpdf.alt5Desc1: PDF.ai takes minimalism to its logical endpoint. The tool does one thing: you upload a PDF and ask questions. There are no multi-format parsers, no citation highlighting systems, no multilingual interfaces, and no AI model selection. This simplicity is both its strength and its limitation.
altsChatpdf.alt5Desc2: For users who only need occasional, basic PDF Q&A, PDF.ai gets out of the way and lets you work. But development has slowed, and the feature set has not meaningfully evolved since launch. For anything beyond basic PDF chat, other alternatives provide substantially more value.
altsChatpdf.alt5BestFor: Users who want absolute simplicity, occasional PDF questions
altsChatpdf.alt6Title: ChatDOC — Best for Tables and Data Extraction
altsChatpdf.alt6Desc1: ChatDOC differentiates itself with strong table extraction and structured data handling. If your documents are heavy on tables, charts, and structured data, ChatDOC better preserves those structures during analysis. The tool supports PDF and DOCX files with particular attention to tabular content.
altsChatpdf.alt6Desc2: ChatDOC offers page-level citations that show the relevant section of the document. The interface is available in English and Chinese. Free users get 2 documents per day with 20 questions each. The Pro plan starts at $5.99 per month. For documents where table accuracy is critical, ChatDOC is worth evaluating, though DocTalk XLSX support may serve similar needs for spreadsheet-heavy workflows.
altsChatpdf.alt6BestFor: Users working with table-heavy documents, data extraction tasks
altsChatpdf.alt7Desc1: Sharly (formerly Sharly AI) focuses on document summarization as its primary use case. Rather than a pure Q&A interface, Sharly emphasizes generating comprehensive summaries of uploaded documents. The tool supports PDFs and offers different summary lengths and styles to match your needs.
altsChatpdf.alt7Desc2: Sharly is a good choice if your primary need is getting an overview of long documents rather than asking specific questions. The summarization quality is generally strong. However, for detailed Q&A with source verification, tools like DocTalk with citation highlighting are more appropriate. Sharly also lacks the multi-format support and multilingual interface found in DocTalk.
altsChatpdf.alt7BestFor: Document summarization, getting quick overviews of long files
altsChatpdf.chooseTitle: How to Choose the Right ChatPDF Alternative
altsChatpdf.chooseDescription: The best alternative depends on your specific needs. Here is a quick decision framework:
altsChatpdf.faq1Question: What is the best free ChatPDF alternative?
altsChatpdf.faq1Answer: Google NotebookLM is the best completely free alternative, offering multi-source notebooks and audio podcast generation with no usage limits (Google account required). DocTalk also offers a free demo with no signup required and a free tier with 500 credits per month.
altsChatpdf.faq2Question: Which ChatPDF alternative supports the most file formats?
altsChatpdf.faq2Answer: DocTalk supports the widest range of document formats with 7 types: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, Markdown, and web URLs. ChatPDF and most other alternatives only support PDF files.
altsChatpdf.faq3Question: Which alternative has the best citation system?
altsChatpdf.faq3Answer: DocTalk has the most advanced citation system with real-time visual highlighting. Click any citation to instantly scroll to and highlight the exact source passage in your document. Other tools only provide page number references.
altsChatpdf.faq4Question: Is there a ChatPDF alternative with team features?
altsChatpdf.faq4Answer: Humata offers the best team collaboration features with shared workspaces, role management, and a Team plan at $49/user/month. Most other alternatives, including DocTalk, focus on individual users.

useCasesStudents.challenge.title: The Academic Reading Challenge
useCasesStudents.challenge.p1: Academic research demands an enormous volume of reading. A typical PhD student reads over 100 papers per year, and that number climbs sharply during literature review phases. Each paper averages 20 to 50 pages of dense, technical prose. Manually reviewing a single paper takes one to three hours, depending on the subject complexity and your familiarity with the field.
useCasesStudents.challenge.p2: The challenge extends beyond sheer volume. Researchers need to extract specific data points from papers: methodology details, statistical results, key findings, and how those findings relate to other work in the field. Skimming is risky because missing a critical caveat in a methodology section can undermine an entire literature review.
useCasesStudents.challenge.p3: Undergraduate students face a different but related challenge. They are often assigned textbook chapters, supplementary readings, and academic articles on topics they are still learning. Without deep domain expertise, parsing academic language is slow and frustrating. Exam preparation compounds the pressure, requiring rapid comprehension across multiple chapters and papers.
useCasesStudents.challenge.p4: Traditional AI chatbots can help with general questions, but they have a critical flaw for academic work: they generate answers from training data, not from your specific document. If an AI hallucinates a statistic or misquotes a finding, and you include it in your paper, your credibility is at stake. Academic research requires verifiable, source-grounded answers.

Clicking citation [1] scrolls to page 42 where the author states their primary finding. Clicking [3] jumps to page 47 where the author discusses implications. The student grasps the thesis's core argument in two minutes.

useCasesStudents.realWorld.methodology.p: Methodology sections are often the most challenging part of a research paper, especially for students new to a field. useCasesStudents.realWorld.examPrep.p2: Every answer includes citations that point back to the textbook pages. useCasesStudents.citations.title: Why Citations Matter for Academic Work
useCasesStudents.citations.p1: The rise of AI tools in academia has brought a serious problem: hallucination. General-purpose AI chatbots generate answers from their training data, not from the specific document you are studying. They may present fabricated statistics, misattribute findings to the wrong authors, or invent citations that do not exist. In academic work, where every claim must be verifiable, this is unacceptable.
useCasesStudents.citations.p2a: You cannot write in a research paper that "an AI said so." Every claim needs a traceable source. DocTalk's
useCasesStudents.citations.p2b: solves this by grounding every AI answer in your actual document text. Each numbered citation corresponds to a specific passage in your uploaded paper. Click the citation, and the document viewer scrolls to the exact text and highlights it.
useCasesStudents.citations.p3: This means DocTalk is not a replacement for reading. It is an accelerator. It helps you find the right passages faster, understand complex sections more quickly, and verify every claim before you include it in your own work. The AI acts as a research assistant that always shows its sources.
useCasesStudents.citations.p4: For students concerned about academic integrity, this distinction is crucial. Using DocTalk to locate and understand source material in a paper you have legitimately accessed is no different from using a search function or index. The tool helps you find information; the understanding and analysis remain yours.

useCasesStudents.multilingual.p1: Academic research is a global endeavor. Groundbreaking papers are published in Chinese, Japanese, German, Spanish, and dozens of other languages. A researcher studying manufacturing techniques may need to review Japanese engineering papers. A historian might analyze German-language primary sources. A medical researcher could encounter Chinese clinical trials.

This breaks down language barriers that have historically limited cross-cultural academic collaboration.
useCasesStudents.multilingual.p3: The citation system works across languages. When DocTalk cites a passage from a Japanese paper, clicking the citation highlights the original Japanese text in the document viewer. You can verify the AI's interpretation against the source, even if you are not fully fluent in the document's language.

useCasesHr.challenge.title: The HR Document Challenge
useCasesHr.challenge.p1: Human resources departments manage some of the most frequently referenced documents in any organization. Employee handbooks, benefits guides, leave policies, code of conduct documents, and employment contracts collectively define the rules that govern every employee's working life. These documents are typically lengthy, updated periodically, and contain interconnected provisions that reference each other.
useCasesHr.challenge.p2: HR professionals field a constant stream of questions about these documents. "How many PTO days do I accrue after two years?" "What is the policy on remote work for my department?" "Does my non-compete apply if I move to a different state?" Each question requires locating the correct document, finding the relevant section, and interpreting the policy language accurately. Answering incorrectly can have real consequences, from employee grievances to compliance violations.
useCasesHr.challenge.p3: Employment contracts add another dimension. Each employee may have slightly different terms, and reviewing contracts during onboarding, promotions, or separations requires careful attention to specific clauses. Non-compete provisions, severance terms, intellectual property assignments, and confidentiality agreements all need precise reading.
useCasesHr.challenge.p4: Compliance requirements make accuracy non-negotiable. HR policies must align with labor law, and the answers HR provides to employees must accurately reflect the documented policies. A tool that accelerates document lookup while maintaining traceability to the source policy is valuable for any HR operation.
